name: VSCP LAW
description_short: Philadelphia personal injury law firm representing catastrophic and fatal injury victims on a contingency fee basis — no upfront costs, free consultations.
description_long:
VSCP LAW is a Philadelphia-based personal injury law firm whose name reflects its four founding partners: Joshua Van Naarden, Gregory Spizer, Ryan Chase, and John Pinto. The firm operates from its offices at 2001 Market Street in Center City Philadelphia and focuses exclusively on catastrophic and fatal injury litigation in Pennsylvania. While some third-party directories list a founding year of 1970, this appears to be a data error unrelated to the VSCP entity as currently constituted — the founding partners' individual careers date to the mid-2000s. The firm holds no CDFI, HUD, or NFCC certifications, as those financial credentials are irrelevant to a litigation practice.

VSCP LAW handles complex personal injury and wrongful death matters across a defined set of practice areas: medical malpractice (surgical errors, delayed diagnosis, hospital negligence), birth injuries (cerebral palsy, delivery complications), cancer misdiagnosis (lung and breast cancer), auto and commercial truck accidents, construction site injuries, slip and fall cases, traumatic brain injuries, product liability, and workplace accidents. The firm explicitly declines minor injury cases, positioning itself for high-stakes litigation where the damages justify intensive legal resources. All representation is on a contingency fee basis — clients pay nothing unless the firm wins or settles the case. Free initial consultations are offered. The specific contingency percentage is not disclosed on their public website.

VSCP LAW's most prominent credential is its attorneys' recognition by Pennsylvania Super Lawyers. All eight eligible attorneys were named Pennsylvania Super Lawyers in 2023, and 12 attorneys appeared on either the Super Lawyers or Rising Stars lists in 2025 — an unusually broad recognition rate for a firm of this size. Founding partner Greg Spizer holds Top 100 status for both Philadelphia and Pennsylvania. Collectively, the founding partners bring approximately 200 combined years of trial experience, and across their careers the team has recovered over $1 billion for clients.

VSCP LAW is a legitimate, highly credentialed personal injury firm with a clear niche: serious, complex cases involving permanent or fatal injuries. Its strengths are real — elite Super Lawyers recognition, a substantial recovery record, and a contingency model that eliminates financial risk for clients. The limitations are structural: the firm does not take minor injury claims, does not provide any financial services, and its BBB status is unverified. It is also worth noting that VSCP LAW does not belong in a consumer finance directory — it is a litigation firm, not a lender, credit counselor, or debt services provider. Consumers researching credit or debt products should look elsewhere; those who have suffered serious injuries in Pennsylvania will find this firm among the more accomplished options in the state.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contingency fee basis — clients owe nothing unless the firm wins or settles the case. No upfront fees of any kind.

pros: $1 billion+ recovered for clients across founding partners' collective careers
All 8 eligible attorneys named Pennsylvania Super Lawyers in 2023 — unusually high recognition rate for the firm's size
12 attorneys on Super Lawyers or Rising Stars lists in 2025
Greg Spizer holds Top 100 status for both Philadelphia and Pennsylvania
~200 combined years of trial experience among founding partners
Contingency fee model — zero cost to clients unless the firm wins or settles
cons: Does not accept minor injury cases — exclusively handles catastrophic and fatal injury claims
Specific contingency fee percentage is not disclosed publicly on their website
BBB listing not found — accreditation and rating status unverified
No confirmed mobile app or branded online client portal
Not a financial services provider — entirely mismatched with consumer finance directory categories
best_for: Victims of catastrophic accidents (auto, truck, construction) seeking experienced trial representation
Medical malpractice victims including surgical errors, misdiagnosis, and birth injury cases
Families pursuing wrongful death claims against negligent individuals or institutions
Serious injury victims in Pennsylvania who cannot afford upfront legal fees
diagnosis: VSCP LAW is best suited for individuals and families facing high-stakes personal injury or wrongful death litigation in Pennsylvania — particularly in medical malpractice and catastrophic accident contexts where the firm's deep trial experience and Super Lawyers-recognized attorneys add meaningful value. The primary caveat is that this is a litigation firm, not a financial services provider; anyone looking for credit repair, lending, or debt relief should look elsewhere entirely.
services: Medical malpractice litigation (surgical errors, misdiagnosis, hospital negligence)
Birth injury cases (cerebral palsy, delivery complications)
Cancer misdiagnosis claims (lung cancer, breast cancer)
Truck and commercial vehicle crash litigation
